text: Implementing security is essential in modern web development. One fundamental measure is using HTTPS, which encrypts data exchanged between users and servers, preventing eavesdropping. Authentication ensures that only authorized users can access sensitive areas, often through passwords or multi-factor verification. Input validation is equally critical; it checks that user-provided data, such as form entries, meets expected formats and rejects malicious inputs. Without these protections, websites risk common vulnerabilities like cross-site scripting (XSS) or SQL injection, where attackers inject harmful code. By combining HTTPS, strong authentication, and thorough input validation, developers can significantly reduce threats and build more trustworthy applications.
